claim: PlanSafeBench-EVM is anchored in real Ethereum transaction templates
recommended_wording: anchored in 220 unique Level-2 Ethereum transaction templates across selected high-risk EVM action categories

claim: User intent/private policy are real user data extracted from blockchain
recommended_wording: user intent, private policy, and AI-generated plans are controlled benchmark constructs

claim: The framework includes a computational security model
recommended_wording: a deterministic pre-signing semantic-policy validation model over intent, policy, plan, and context

claim: Text classifiers solve EVM transaction-plan safety
recommended_wording: text classifiers are baselines; counterfactual and LLM audits motivate explicit validation

claim: Schema-valid LLM output is sufficient for safety
recommended_wording: structured-output compliance alone is insufficient for transaction safety
expert-reviewed taxonomy and decision-boundary rules; no multi-expert row-level annotation claimed

claim: The validator is an independent human judge
recommended_wording: the validator is a deterministic semantic-policy compliance audit
